name: Cccs of Greater Greensboro
description_short: Family Service of the Piedmont is a private non-profit providing free and low-cost financial counseling, credit advice, and housing assistance alongside domestic violence, mental health, and family support services.
description_long:
Family Service of the Piedmont has operated for over 80 years as a private, not-for-profit agency serving the Greensboro area. The organization addresses multiple dimensions of family stability, recognizing that financial distress often intersects with domestic violence, mental health challenges, and child welfare concerns. They serve more than 26,000 children and adults annually across five core service areas.

Their financial stability services specifically include budget and credit counseling, debt management planning, foreclosure prevention, and housing counseling with educational components. The organization employs certified financial counselors who work with clients on practical money management, credit repair strategies, and long-term financial stability. Their approach integrates financial counseling within a holistic framework that acknowledges clients' broader life circumstances, including trauma, job loss, and family transitions.

Family Service of the Piedmont distinguishes itself by treating financial instability as part of a larger ecosystem of family challenges rather than an isolated problem. Case studies on their website show clients receiving coordinated support—for example, a laid-off worker receiving job transition counseling alongside financial planning, or a domestic violence survivor receiving both shelter and financial independence coaching. This integrated model reflects their 80-year institutional commitment to "building safe and healthy families."

The organization's primary limitation is geographic scope—they serve the Piedmont region specifically, making them unavailable to consumers outside their service area. While their website emphasizes free and low-cost services, specific pricing structures and income eligibility thresholds are not detailed online. Additionally, as a full-service family services agency, their consumer credit counseling may be one of many competing internal priorities rather than their primary specialization.
pros: Offers free or low-cost budget and credit counseling as part of non-profit mission
Provides foreclosure prevention and housing counseling to help clients avoid home loss
80+ year track record serving families in the Piedmont region with institutional stability
Integrates financial counseling with domestic violence support, mental health services, and parenting education for holistic client care
Debt management and financial education designed to build long-term stability, not quick fixes
Specialized housing counseling supporting clients facing mortgage challenges and financial transitions
Serves 26,000+ clients annually, indicating significant community trust and capacity
cons: Geographic limitation—only serves the Piedmont region; unavailable to consumers outside service area
Specific pricing, income eligibility requirements, and fee structures not clearly published on website
Consumer credit counseling is one service among many (domestic violence, mental health, child welfare), potentially not their primary specialty
No indication of NFCC certification or specific credit counseling credentials for staff mentioned on website
best_for: Greensboro/Piedmont area residents experiencing job loss or financial instability
Domestic violence survivors seeking both shelter and financial independence planning
Homeowners facing mortgage difficulties, foreclosure risk, or post-loss housing challenges
Low-income families seeking integrated financial counseling alongside parenting or mental health support
diagnosis: Family Service of the Piedmont is best for Piedmont-area residents combining financial instability with domestic violence, mental health challenges, or family crises who benefit from integrated rather than siloed counseling. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation—this organization is not accessible to consumers outside the Piedmont region, and their consumer credit focus is secondary to their broader family services mission.
services: Domestic violence shelter and safety planning
Mental health and substance use counseling
Financial stability coaching for crisis situations
Advocacy and court navigation for domestic violence survivors
Child and family therapy for trauma and abuse survivors
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
meta_description: Family Service of the Piedmont offers free financial counseling, credit advice, and housing assistance with mental health and family support.
